metadata.summary: A simple page cache for Wagtail based on the Django cache middleware.

documentation:
Wagtail Cache
=============

A fast and simple page cache for Wagtail, inspired by the Django cache
middleware.

[Documentation](https://docs.coderedcorp.com/wagtail-cache/) |
[Source code on GitHub](https://github.com/coderedcorp/wagtail-cache) |
[PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/wagtail-cache/)


Status
------

|                        |                      |
|------------------------|----------------------|
| Python Package         | [![PyPI - Python Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/wagtail-cache)](https://pypi.org/project/wagtail-cache/) [![PyPI - Wheel](https://img.shields.io/pypi/wheel/wagtail-cache)](https://pypi.org/project/wagtail-cache/) [![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/wagtail-cache)](https://pypi.org/project/wagtail-cache/) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/wagtail-cache)](https://pypi.org/project/wagtail-cache/) |
| Build                  | [![Build Status](https://dev.azure.com/coderedcorp/cr-github/_apis/build/status/wagtail-cache?branchName=main)](https://dev.azure.com/coderedcorp/cr-github/_build/latest?definitionId=9&branchName=main) [![Azure DevOps tests (branch)](https://img.shields.io/azure-devops/tests/coderedcorp/cr-github/9/main)](https://dev.azure.com/coderedcorp/cr-github/_build/latest?definitionId=9&branchName=main) [![Azure DevOps coverage (branch)](https://img.shields.io/azure-devops/coverage/coderedcorp/cr-github/9/main)](https://dev.azure.com/coderedcorp/cr-github/_build/latest?definitionId=9&branchName=main) |


Quick Start
-----------

Follow the [Installation Guide](https://docs.coderedcorp.com/wagtail-cache/stable/getting_started/install.html)


Why Wagtail Cache?
------------------

Django has a robust cache middleware that already has the functionality
needed to cache web pages effectively. But turning the cache middleware
on will blindly cache every request and does not work well with a Wagtail site.

Wagtail Cache provides a middleware, decorator, and mixin that works well with
Wagtail pages, Django views, or even manually on any request/response to
efficiently cache and serve from cache.

The end result is ultra-fast page serving that requires zero database hits
to serve cached pages. Other solutions such as template caching still require
database hits for Wagtail to serve a page.

Wagtail Cache also does not require any additional infrastructure such as Redis,
Memcached, proxies, etc. It can work directly off the filesystem, or using any
of Django's built-in cache backends.


Contributing
------------

Follow the [contributing guide](https://docs.coderedcorp.com/wagtail-cache/stable/contributing.html)


Attribution
-----------

Icon file "wagtailcache-bolt.svg":

* Was sourced from the Fork Awesome project at
   https://github.com/ForkAwesome/Fork-Awesome.

* Is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 3.0 Unported license,
   a copy of which is available at https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/3.0/

* Has been modified from the original sources.

documentation: CLI tool to clear wagtailcache.

modules.name: wagtailcache.cache
documentation: Functionality to set, serve from, and clear the cache.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache._patch_header
documentation: Adds our Cache Control status to the response headers.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache._delete_vary_cookie
documentation:
Deletes the `Vary: Cookie` header while keeping other items of the
Vary header in tact. Inspired by `django.utils.cache.patch_vary_headers`.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache._chop_querystring
documentation: Given a request object, remove any of our ignored querystrings from it.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache._chop_cookies
documentation: If the request contains cookies which are not native to Django, remove them.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache._chop_response_vary
documentation:
In many situations Django adds `Vary: Cookie`, however, nearly all
tracking software (Google, HubSpot, etc.) litters worthless crap into the
cookies which ends up busting our cache, making it effectively impossible to
cache anything. With this special setting, we are going to forcibly remove
the `Vary: Cookie` header unless the cookie contains a recognizable Django
session or CSRF token.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache._get_cache_key
documentation:
Wrapper for Django's get_cache_key which first strips specific
querystrings. Since the Django cache middleware is somewhat complicated and
RFC compliant, we are best off to chop and pass it along rather than
re-inventing the cache keying logic.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache._learn_cache_key
documentation:
Wrapper for Django's learn_cache_key which first strips specific
querystrings. Since the Django cache middleware is somewhat complicated and
RFC compliant, we are best off to chop and pass it along rather than
re-inventing the cache keying logic.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache.clear_cache
documentation:
Clears the Wagtail cache backend.

```
param urls

An optional list of strings, representing regular expressions
```

to match against the list of URLs in the cache. If a URL matches, it is
deleted from the cache. If `urls` is `None` the entire cache is cleared.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache.cache_page
documentation:
Decorator that determines whether or not to cache a page or serve a cached
page.

functions.name: wagtailcache.cache.nocache_page
documentation: Decorator that sets no-cache on all responses.

classes.name: wagtailcache.cache.CacheControl
documentation: ``Cache-Control`` header values.

classes.name: wagtailcache.cache.Status
documentation: WAGTAIL_CACHE_HEADER header values.

classes.name: wagtailcache.cache.FetchFromCacheMiddleware
documentation:
Loads a request from the cache if it exists.
Mostly stolen from `django.middleware.cache.FetchFromCacheMiddleware`.

classes.name: wagtailcache.cache.UpdateCacheMiddleware
documentation:
Saves a response to the cache.
Mostly stolen from `django.middleware.cache.UpdateCacheMiddleware`.

classes.name: wagtailcache.cache.WagtailCacheMixin

methods.name: wagtailcache.cache.WagtailCacheMixin.serve_password_required_response
documentation: Add a cache-control header if the page requires a password.

methods.name: wagtailcache.cache.WagtailCacheMixin.serve
documentation:
Add a custom cache-control header, or set to private if the page is
being served behind a view restriction.
Add cache-control headers to various Page responses that could be returned.
